title: Challenge- IAM- Access Control to Users Services

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to launch the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab.
On the login page: Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit). Input the provided IAM Username and Password. Click Sign In.
Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit).
Input the provided IAM Username and Password.
Click Sign In.
In the Console, select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

XYZ Company needs to manage access for its employees to use AWS services securely. Your job is to create IAM users for team members, organize them into groups, assign permissions to the groups, and validate the setup. IAM lets you control who can use AWS and what they can do, like viewing EC2 or billing details. In this task, we are going to create new IAM users by providing the name, password access, permissions, and tags, and add them to their respective groups. Here’s how to do each step super simply:
Create IAM Users: In IAM, Go to Users Create 4 users ex:(2 for development, 2 for HR) Set username and password for them
In IAM, Go to Users
Create 4 users ex:(2 for development, 2 for HR)
Set username and password for them
Create IAM Groups: In IAM, Go to permissions Create 2 groups ex:(Development, HR) For Dev-team give EC2Readonly and S3ReadOnly access For HR-Team give Billing access and create 2 groups
In IAM, Go to permissions
Create 2 groups ex:(Development, HR)
For Dev-team give EC2Readonly and S3ReadOnly access
For HR-Team give Billing access and create 2 groups
Make sure everything is correct. In the lab console, click Validate to check if the IAM users, groups, tags, and permissions are set up right.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
In the lab console, click End Challenge and wait for it to finish.
You’ve completed the IAM setup challenge!
